titel: Reduced taxes on income from employment, pensions, and sickness and disability benefits.
html:
This proposal suggests increasing the standard tax allowance for employment income by a total of 17.36 billion Swedish krona. This tax reduction primarily benefits full-time employees with low to medium incomes. The proposal means that taxes will, on average, decrease by approximately 3,800 krona per person per year for those affected.

Furthermore, the proposal suggests increasing the basic allowance for individuals who have reached the age of 66 by a total of 4.04 billion Swedish krona. This means that taxes will, on average, decrease by approximately 2,300 krona per person per year for those affected.

The proposal also includes suggestions for tax reductions for individuals with income in the form of disability and activity benefits. The purpose of this proposal is to eliminate the difference in taxation of such benefits compared to earned income, which exists in certain income brackets. The proposal means that the tax reduction for disability and activity benefits will be increased for individuals with an income base exceeding approximately 54,000 krona per year. Individuals with such income are expected to receive an average tax reduction of 1,800 krona per year.

The proposed changes are scheduled to come into effect on January 1, 2026.

Proposed Resolutions for the Parliament

Prop. 2025/26:32

Government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Income Tax Act (1999:1229).
  2. The Parliament approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2024:1140) amending the Act (2023:758) amending the Act (2022:887) amending the Income Tax Act (1999:1229).

3.

Prop. 2025/26:32
tldr: The government wants to lower taxes for many people in Sweden. The proposal means that those who work full-time, are pensioners, or receive disability benefits will have more money each year.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a plan to reduce taxes on various types of income. For those who work full-time and have low to medium incomes, the proposed reduction would, on average, result in an extra 3,800 Swedish krona per year. This is achieved by modifying what is known as the "workplace tax deduction," which is a portion of the tax deducted from your salary.

The proposal also aims to make it more affordable for older people to pay taxes. For those who have reached the age of 66 at the beginning of the year, the proposed reduction would, on average, provide an extra 2,300 Swedish krona per person per year. This is achieved by increasing the basic allowance for older individuals, meaning that a larger portion of their income will not be taxed.

The proposal also includes tax reductions for individuals who receive disability benefits or activity benefits. The goal is to ensure that the tax on these benefits is equivalent to the tax on regular salaries for certain income levels. Those with benefits exceeding approximately 54,000 Swedish krona per year may receive an additional 1,800 Swedish krona per year.

If the parliament votes in favor of the proposal, the new rules will come into effect on January 1, 2026. The proposal is a so-called "proposition," which is an official proposal from the government to the parliament.
